{"id":"project-LICHEN-m4h2","title":"SecureDatagramChannel pending_requests race on concurrent response handling","description":"File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:346-347

In _unprotect(), the check-then-pop on pending_requests dict is not atomic:

    if is_response and msg.token in peer_ctx.pending_requests:
        request_id = peer_ctx.pending_requests.pop(msg.token)

Multiple _process_incoming() tasks can run concurrently (line 289 creates a new task per datagram). If two responses with the same token arrive simultaneously:

1. Task A: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
2. Task B: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
3. Task A: pop(msg.token) -> succeeds
4. Task B: pop(msg.token) -> KeyError (key no longer exists)

The pending_requests dict is accessed without synchronization, and the context_store lock only protects getting the PeerContext reference, not subsequent access to its fields.

This could cause crashes when processing duplicate/retransmitted responses or during network congestion with multiple in-flight responses.

Fix: Use dict.pop(key, None) instead of the check-then-pop pattern, or add per-context synchronization for pending_requests access.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T16:56:44.890012Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T16:56:44.890058Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-m4h2","title":"SecureDatagramChannel pending_requests race on concurrent response handling","description":"File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:346-347

In _unprotect(), the check-then-pop on pending_requests dict is not atomic:

    if is_response and msg.token in peer_ctx.pending_requests:
        request_id = peer_ctx.pending_requests.pop(msg.token)

Multiple _process_incoming() tasks can run concurrently (line 289 creates a new task per datagram). If two responses with the same token arrive simultaneously:

1. Task A: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
2. Task B: checks msg.token in pending_requests -> True
3. Task A: pop(msg.token) -> succeeds
4. Task B: pop(msg.token) -> KeyError (key no longer exists)

The pending_requests dict is accessed without synchronization, and the context_store lock only protects getting the PeerContext reference, not subsequent access to its fields.

This could cause crashes when processing duplicate/retransmitted responses or during network congestion with multiple in-flight responses.

Fix: Use dict.pop(key, None) instead of the check-then-pop pattern, or add per-context synchronization for pending_requests access.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T16:56:44.890012Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:11:15.565259251Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:11:15.565259251Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:16:15.565259251Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:11:15.565259251Z"}

new_value: Replaced check-then-pop with atomic .pop(token, None) in _unprotect and _send_protected; removed outdated comment. No more KeyError on concurrent responses. Verified in test_coap and secure channel tests.
